pages.title: About this decision site
pages.description: Why this site exists and what problem it is trying to solve for Florida homeowners after the report arrives.
pages.quickAnswer: Use this site when the report has arrived and you still need a safer next step before you call contractors or sign anything.
pages.intro: The point of this site is to turn a confusing report into one clear next move without pretending to be the program.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume this site is the program.
Do not assume the goal is broad eligibility traffic.
Do not assume contractor routing comes before recommendation clarity.
bodySections: It focuses on the gap between the inspection report, the first project choice, and the first real quote.
Each page is supposed to answer one homeowner question at a time and then send you back to the main route.
The site checks current official program pages before it updates route copy or expands a claim.
It is not a contractor directory dressed up as advice.

pages.title: Methodology
pages.description: How the site handles current rules, source freshness, and route promotion discipline.
pages.quickAnswer: This site checks current official MSFH pages first, shows the source stack on each route, and stays narrow when the rule support is weak or stale.
pages.intro: Newer official pages and support articles win. Older PDFs are background only.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume old PDFs outrank current support articles.
Do not assume a held route should auto-promote because it exists.
Do not assume stale source support is safe enough for expansion.

primaryCta.label: See what the program may pay for

secondaryCta.label: Go back to the report decision path

bodySections: Every route keeps the official sources that support it, plus a review date so stale pages can be caught quickly.
If the rule support is unclear or stale, the page should stay narrow or hold back instead of expanding claims.
Program rules are kept separate from contractor and quote guidance so the site does not imply approval.
Support-state pages stay cautious because portal language can change faster than project rules.

pages.title: Contact
pages.description: How to reach the site operator without implying program affiliation.
pages.quickAnswer: Use this contact path for editorial corrections or a paid pilot conversation tied to one improvement route, not for official program case handling.
pages.intro: Use this contact page for site feedback, factual corrections, or a paid pilot inquiry tied to one improvement-specific route.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume this inbox can resolve an official application issue.
Do not assume partner outreach belongs on broad generic pages.
Do not assume a route should stay live if its source stack changed.

primaryCta.label: Return to the contractor quote route

bodySections: This inbox is for the editorial product, not for official program case management.
If a page appears stale or unclear, send the route path and the source that changed so the next review can happen quickly.
Licensed Florida contractors can use this path for a paid pilot conversation when the fit stays narrow around one route, one scope discipline, and clear county coverage.
Commercial conversations should stay tied to improvement-specific routes instead of broad directory placement.

pages.title: Not government affiliated
pages.description: A plain-language disclosure that this site is not the program and cannot guarantee approval or reimbursement.
pages.quickAnswer: This is an independent editorial and routing site, not the program, the State of Florida, or an approval channel.
pages.intro: This site is an independent editorial and routing product. It is not the My Safe Florida Home program, the State of Florida, or an official case-management channel.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume any page here can approve a grant.
Do not assume any route guarantees reimbursement.
Do not assume contractor language on this site overrides official rules.

primaryCta.label: Read what to do after the report

secondaryCta.label: See how the site verifies rules

bodySections: The site does not approve grants, assign inspections, or decide reimbursement.
Any contractor or checklist guidance here should still be verified against your report and the current official program rules.
No page should be read as a promise of approval, reimbursement, or savings.

pages.title: Privacy
pages.description: Privacy terms for route guidance, partner inquiries, and event logging.
pages.quickAnswer: The site stores submitted form fields and route-event data only for route guidance, editorial review, partner pilot review, and limited follow-up tied to this product.
pages.intro: The site collects the fields you submit in homeowner and partner forms, plus route-event data used to understand which decision paths are useful and where route copy needs review.
whatNotToAssume: Do not submit account credentials, grant portal logins, Social Security numbers, insurance policy numbers, or permit documents.
Do not assume stored route data is the same thing as official program data.
Do not assume a partner inquiry creates placement, endorsement, or guaranteed volume.
bodySections: The site stores the form fields you enter, including contact details, route context, and the message or route focus you submit.
The site also stores route-event data such as CTA clicks and form opens so the route logic can be reviewed and improved.
Submissions are used for next-step guidance, editorial corrections, route QA, and route-specific partner pilot review. They are not used for official program case handling.
This phase-1 build uses file-backed operational storage. Access should stay limited to the operator and authorized service providers needed to host or maintain the site.
If you need a correction or deletion review for data you submitted, use the contact path and identify the email address used in the submission so the request can be reviewed manually.
Some page assets or infrastructure providers may receive standard browser request data such as IP address, user agent, and referrer when the page loads.

pages.title: Terms
pages.description: Usage terms for the independent editorial product and paid pilot inquiry lane.
pages.quickAnswer: Use the site as an independent decision aid, not as legal, insurance, permitting, contractor, or official program advice.
pages.intro: The site provides route guidance built from current public materials and internal editorial logic. It does not replace official program instructions or professional advice.
whatNotToAssume: Do not assume any page guarantees eligibility, reimbursement, placement, or contractor volume.
Do not assume partner pilot review creates endorsement, exclusivity, or approval.
Do not assume route copy replaces permit, insurance, pricing, or legal review.
bodySections: Use the site as a decision aid only. Verify project choices against your report, current official materials, permit rules, insurer requirements, and contractor documents.
You are responsible for the information you submit and for the decisions you make after reading the site.
Commercial routing and partner review stay discretionary and route-specific. No page or inquiry creates a promise of placement, exclusivity, or lead volume.
The site may change route copy, source support, pricing, and pilot availability as the program or editorial review changes.
Questions about privacy, corrections, or route-specific issues should be sent through the contact path.
